pulse: <cite index="1-2,1-19">Stocks edged higher as investors monitored U.S.-Iran negotiations, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.55% to close at 754.6</cite>. <cite index="1-8">Dell Technologies surged nearly 30% on strong AI server demand, though broad tech gains remain modest</cite>. Nasdaq 100 (QQQ) climbed 3.15% over the week as <cite index="9-7">AI software and infrastructure stocks including Microsoft were sharply higher with gains between 3% and 4%</cite>.

why_paras: Today's market: <cite index="9-4">Washington and Tehran agreed to a 60-day memorandum to extend the ceasefire and gradually restore energy exports from the Persian Gulf</cite>. Relief rally in oil-sensitive sectors offset by cautious tone in chip stocks following the week's strong rally.

Why it happened: <cite index="2-5,2-6">Hopes that a ceasefire deal could pave the way for an end to the Iran conflict drove stocks toward a historic streak of weekly gains, with the market buoyed by the artificial-intelligence trade; the S&P 500 surged 20% from war-driven lows on track for its ninth straight weekly advance</cite>. However, <cite index="4-11,4-13">analyst Eric Parnell warned that current valuations of many semiconductor stocks have become extremely frothy and bust cycles have historically followed boom cycles</cite>.

What it means: The market has climbed 6.03% in May on geopolitical relief and AI tailwinds, but breadth is weakening (5/11 sectors green per data). Valuations in semis like MU and AMD are extreme. For Singapore investors, the 9-week rally has created a rotation opportunity: hold quality mega-cap tech (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) and avoid commodity-priced semiconductors near 52-week highs.
macro: <cite index="9-6">Jobless claims and consumer spending remained robust, while PCE inflation rose to a three-year high</cite>—growth remains solid but inflation stickiness limits Fed rate cuts.

<cite index="2-6,2-8">The S&P 500 has surged roughly 20% from war-driven lows and is on track for its longest weekly win streak since 2023</cite>—technically extended but macro backdrop supportive if geopolitical risks ease.

VIX at 16.3 shows calm; 10Y Treasury at 4.48% signals patient capital. However, breadth (5/11 sectors green) and analyst caution on semiconductor multiples suggest consolidation risk if earnings revisions slow.
opportunity_radar:
• AI Infrastructure (but avoid peak valuation semiconductors) — AMD surged 4.55% today on strong data-center momentum. OpenAI and Meta committed 6 GW each worth about $60 billion in multi-year deployments. But AMD at 518.09 (351.5% YTD) and MU at 923.52 (836% YTD) are priced for perfection. Pick infrastructure plays (MSFT +3.47% today, AVGO +1.12%) or foundry (TSM) instead. (e.g. MSFT, AVGO, TSMC—not AMD or MU)
  • Iran ceasefire optionality (Energy & Industrials relief) — U.S. and Iran agreed to a 60-day memorandum to extend ceasefire and gradually restore energy exports. Cheaper energy benefits consumer discretionary and manufacturing. Real Estate and Utilities lagged today (–0.49% and –1.13%), but energy demand recovery could drive utility dividends and capital allocation. (e.g. Watch energy-heavy dividend stocks; avoid rate-sensitive utilities until 10Y stabilizes below 4.4%)
  • Earnings quality over momentum (Magnificent Seven defensible) — AI software and infrastructure patrons including Microsoft, Oracle, and Palantir gained 3–4% vs. hardware names. Microsoft (426.99, –6.95% YTD) and Apple (312.51, +49.68% YTD) remain digestible on fundamentals. Mega-cap breadth shields downside. (e.g. MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL over chipmaker rallies near 52-week highs)

Hold core positions in mega-cap tech and select AI infrastructure plays. The VIX at 16.3 is calm, but valuation breadth (5/11 sectors green, semis at 40–46× forward P/E) argues for rebalancing: lock in chip gains (MU, AMD), trim late-cycle positions (META flat, TSLA +1 month), and rotate into undervalued healthcare (+1.4% today) and quality growth (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL). The Iran ceasefire extension supports a 3–6 month hold for U.S. equities, but do not chase semiconductor highs. The next leg higher requires broadening breadth and earnings confirmation, not geopolitical guesswork.

stw.reason: <cite index="9-7,9-16">Microsoft gained 3–4% today as an AI software and infrastructure patron</cite>. At 426.99 (–6.95% YTD) and a defensive 0.85 beta, it trades below 2026 peaks. Cloud + AI capex visibility extends to 2030; margin expansion and share buybacks de-risk. Buy on any correction toward $400.

However, at 2.69 beta and 351% gains, risk/reward is neutral—hold existing for six more months until MI450 revenue inflection; do not add.

stw.reason: Apple at 312.51 (49.68% YTD, 0.96 beta) closed at 52-week highs today with +0.53% daily gain. Services growth (20%+ CAGR), installed base moat, and dividend yield (1.8%) provide multi-year compounding. Valuation stable vs. NVDA; buy on any dip below $300.

stw.reason: Micron at 923.52 (836% YTD, 45.7× P/E, 2.67 beta) is a peak-cycle trap. <cite index="4-11,4-13">Analyst Eric Parnell warned semiconductor valuations are frothy and bust cycles follow booms</cite>. Exit 100%—HBM prices will compress when hyperscaler capex normalizes in H2 2026. Redeploy to AAPL or MSFT.
